title: DeviceSet Object
description: Definition of a device set in a library. Groups devices with different packages but the same symbol and gate configuration.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: area
properties.description: Bounding area of this device set.

properties.name: description
properties.description: Human-readable description of this device set.

properties.name: devices
properties.description: Gets the collection of devices in this deviceset.

properties.name: gates
properties.description: Gates (logical sub-units) in this device set.

properties.name: headline
properties.description: First line of the description, used as a short title.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: library
properties.description: Name of the library where this device set is defined.

properties.name: libraryLocallyModified
properties.description: True if the source library has been locally modified.

properties.name: libraryVersion
properties.description: Version number of the source library.

properties.name: locallyModified
properties.description: True if this device set has been locally modified.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Assigned name of this device set.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: prefix
properties.description: Prefix used for part designators (e.g., R for resistors).

properties.name: userValue
properties.description: True if this device set allows user-defined values; false if values are fixed.
